titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 1253
GA 1253
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Repeat verse marker for 6.40 added by David Parker.
Word break corrected at 6.66 by Rachel Kevern.
3.32 block marker moved two words backwards by Rachel Kevern.
[εποιει] ει changed to επ[οι]ει at 5.16 by Rachel Kevern.
Lacunose word added in 6.33 by David Parker.
Iota adscripts removed by David Parker.
Word breaks at 5.5 corrected by David Parker.
Word break at 5.16 corrected by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 1253Sinai, Katherinen Kloster, Gr. 303Name of book transcribed: Theophylact on 4 Gospels.First folio transcribed: 184rBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: WJETranscription begun: 11-03-10Transcription finished: 22-03-10 Multiple line break and comm tags added by AB on 16.03.12. A few problems that were picked up by AB were fixed by RK on 22.03.12. RK has not fixed the many other problems spotted in this transcription 21.11.12.Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (>) - No Obeloi (†) - No Other remarks: Folio 193v, Chapter 4, verse 32 βρ should be dotted but I could not do it! (Note from RK: it looks fine – they are dotted.) There is something very strange about folio 209. I suspect the verso and recto have been transposed. 209r is very difficult to read because of the poor state of the ms, and then the text breaks off at Chapter 9, verse 38, so sorry again no Chapter 18 (Note from RK: transcriber has transposed F 209r and 209v in the transcription. To revert them to the order they appear in the images creates problems with the tagging of the verses, so I have left them as they are. FINAL plain text transcription date: 24.04.2103

ab.note: RK – F 209v and F209r have been transposed in the transcription. If they are put back the right way around it will create problems with the tagging of 9.2
